article_6.title: ICT risk management framework
article_6.full_text: Financial entities shall have in place a sound, comprehensive and well-documented ICT risk management framework as part of their overall risk management system.
question_templates: Do you have an ICT risk management framework aligned with DORA requirements?
Does your ICT risk management framework cover identification, protection, detection, response, and recovery capabilities?
Is your ICT risk management framework documented and regularly reviewed?

article_11.title: ICT business continuity policy and ICT response and recovery plans
article_11.full_text: Financial entities shall have in place a sound ICT business continuity policy and ICT response and recovery plans.
question_templates: Can you demonstrate ICT business continuity and disaster recovery capabilities with defined RTOs and RPOs?
Do you have documented ICT response and recovery plans?
Are your business continuity plans tested regularly?
Have you identified critical ICT services and their dependencies?

article_19.title: Classification of ICT-related incidents and cyber threats
article_19.full_text: Financial entities shall classify ICT-related incidents and determine their impact based on criticality criteria.
question_templates: Do you have processes to detect, report, and escalate ICT-related incidents in accordance with DORA timelines?
Can you classify ICT incidents by severity and impact?
Do you maintain records of all ICT-related incidents?
Are major incidents reported within 4 hours initially and 72 hours for intermediate reports?

article_28.title: General principles on the use of ICT third-party service providers
article_28.full_text: Financial entities shall manage ICT third-party risk as an integral component of ICT risk within their ICT risk management framework.
question_templates: Do you have a documented third-party risk management process for ICT services?
Are all ICT third-party service providers subject to due diligence before engagement?
Do you maintain a register of all ICT third-party service providers?
Are third-party contracts reviewed for DORA compliance?

article_29.title: Preliminary assessment of ICT concentration risk at entity level
article_29.full_text: Financial entities shall assess ICT concentration risk in relation to the use of ICT third-party service providers.
question_templates: Do you assess and monitor ICT concentration risk with third-party providers?
Have you identified critical or important ICT third-party service providers?
Do you have contingency plans for concentration risk scenarios?
Are alternative providers identified for critical ICT services?

article_30.title: Key contractual provisions
article_30.full_text: Contractual arrangements on the use of ICT services shall include at minimum specified provisions.
question_templates: Do your ICT third-party contracts include all mandatory DORA provisions?
Are data access, portability, and deletion rights specified in contracts?
Do contracts include audit rights and right to terminate?
Are service level agreements clearly defined with measurable targets?

article_8.title: Identification
article_8.full_text: Financial entities shall identify, classify and adequately document all ICT-supported business functions, roles and responsibilities.
question_templates: Have you identified and documented all ICT-supported business functions?
Are ICT assets classified based on criticality and sensitivity?
Do you maintain an inventory of all ICT assets and dependencies?

article_15.title: Testing of ICT business continuity plans and ICT response and recovery plans
article_15.full_text: Financial entities shall test at appropriate intervals, and at least yearly, their ICT business continuity plans and ICT response and recovery plans.
question_templates: Are ICT business continuity and disaster recovery plans tested at least annually?
Do you conduct advanced testing such as threat-led penetration testing?
Are test results documented and used to improve plans?

article_26.title: Digital operational resilience testing
article_26.full_text: Financial entities shall establish a sound testing programme for ICT tools, systems and processes.
question_templates: Do you have a comprehensive digital operational resilience testing programme?
Are vulnerability assessments conducted regularly?
Do you perform threat-led penetration testing on critical systems?

article_20.title: Governance
article_20.full_text: Member States shall ensure that management bodies of essential and important entities approve cybersecurity risk-management measures.
question_templates: Does your management body approve and oversee cybersecurity risk management measures?
Are cybersecurity risks regularly reported to management?
Has management received appropriate training on cybersecurity risk management?

article_21.title: Cybersecurity risk-management measures
article_21.full_text: Essential and important entities shall take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage cybersecurity risks.
question_templates: Have you implemented comprehensive cybersecurity risk management measures?
Do you have policies for risk analysis and information system security?
Are incident handling and business continuity measures in place?
Do you manage supply chain security risks?

article_22.title: Supply chain security
article_22.full_text: Essential and important entities shall take appropriate measures to address cybersecurity risks arising from the supply chain.
question_templates: Do you assess and manage cybersecurity risks in your supply chain?
Are security requirements included in supplier contracts?
Do you evaluate the overall quality of products and cybersecurity practices of suppliers?
Are critical suppliers subject to enhanced due diligence?

article_23.title: Reporting obligations
article_23.full_text: Essential and important entities shall notify the relevant CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident.
question_templates: Do you have processes to notify authorities of significant cybersecurity incidents?
Can you provide early warning within 24 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident?
Do you submit incident notification within 72 hours?
Is a final report provided within one month of notification?

article_24.title: Use of European cybersecurity certification schemes
article_24.full_text: Essential and important entities may use European cybersecurity certification schemes to demonstrate compliance.
question_templates: Do you hold any European cybersecurity certifications?
Are you pursuing certification under EU cybersecurity schemes?
Do your ICT products and services have relevant certifications?
